1	import csv
2	import io
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
4	from app.extensions import db
5	from app.models.user import User, Permission, TempGrant, Role
6	from app.services.audit_service import log_action
7	
8	
9	def has_permission(user: User, codename: str, org_unit_id: int | None = None) -> bool:
10	    """Check if a user has a permission via roles or active temp grants.
11	
12	    If org_unit_id is provided, also verify the user has access to that org unit
13	    (direct membership or via an ancestor unit).
14	    """
15	    permission_granted = False
16	
17	    for role in user.roles:
18	        if role.permissions.filter_by(codename=codename).first():
19	            permission_granted = True
20	            break
21	
22	    if not permission_granted:
23	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
24	        grant = TempGrant.query.filter_by(
25	            user_id=user.id, is_active=True
26	        ).join(Permission).filter(Permission.codename == codename).first()
27	        if grant:
28	            expires_at = grant.expires_at
29	            if expires_at.tzinfo is None:
30	                expires_at = expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
31	            if expires_at > now:
32	                permission_granted = True
33	
34	    if not permission_granted:
35	        return False
36	
37	    if org_unit_id is None:
38	        return True
39	
40	    # Verify org unit access
41	    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit, UserOrgUnit
42	    user_org_ids = {uo.org_unit_id for uo in UserOrgUnit.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()}
43	
44	    if org_unit_id in user_org_ids:
45	        return True
46	
47	    # Walk up the ancestor chain
48	    current = OrgUnit.query.get(org_unit_id)
49	    while current and current.parent_id:
50	        if current.parent_id in user_org_ids:
51	            return True
52	        current = OrgUnit.query.get(current.parent_id)
53	
54	    return False
55

58	    if role in user.roles:
59	        return
60	    user.roles.append(role)

112	    db.session.commit()

122	def register_for_class(class_id: int, user: User) -> ClassAttendee:
123	    tc = TrainingClass.query.get_or_404(class_id)
124	    if not tc.is_active:
125	        raise ValueError("This class is no longer active")
126	    count = ClassAttendee.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id).count()
127	    if count >= tc.max_attendees:
128	        raise ValueError("This class is full")
129	    existing = ClassAttendee.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id, user_id=user.id).first()
130	    if existing:
131	        raise ValueError("You are already registered for this class")

133	    db.session.add(att)
134	    db.session.commit()

138	def mark_attendance(class_id: int, user_ids: list[int], instructor: User):
139	    tc = TrainingClass.query.get_or_404(class_id)
140	    if tc.instructor_id != instructor.id:
141	        raise PermissionError("Only the instructor can mark attendance")
142	    for uid in user_ids:
143	        att = ClassAttendee.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id, user_id=uid).first()
144	        if att:
145	            att.attended = True

148	
149	def format_reviewer_name(user: User, display_mode: str) -> str:
150	    if display_mode == 'full_name':
151	        return user.full_name or user.username
152	    elif display_mode == 'initials':
153	        name = user.full_name or user.username
154	        parts = name.split()
155	        return '.'.join(p[0].upper() for p in parts if p) + '.'
156	    else:
157	        return 'Anonymous Reviewer'
158	
159	
160	def get_reviews_for_class(class_id: int, display_mode: str = 'anonymous', visible_only: bool = True):
161	    query = ClassReview.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id)
162	    if visible_only:
163	        query = query.filter_by(is_visible=True)
164	    reviews = query.order_by(ClassReview.created_at.desc()).all()
165	    result = []
166	    for r in reviews:
167	        reviewer = User.query.get(r.reviewer_id)
168	        data = r.to_dict()
169	        data['reviewer_display'] = format_reviewer_name(reviewer, display_mode)
170	        data['coach_reply'] = r.coach_reply.to_dict() if r.coach_reply else None
171	        result.append(data)
172	    if reviews:
173	        ratings = [r.rating for r in reviews]
174	        avg = round(sum(ratings) / len(ratings), 1)
175	        dist = {i: ratings.count(i) for i in range(1, 6)}
176	    else:
177	        avg = 0
178	        dist = {i: 0 for i in range(1, 6)}
179	    return {'reviews': result, 'average_rating': avg, 'rating_distribution': dist, 'total': len(reviews)}

16	def scan_review_content(text: str) -> dict:
17	    if not text:
18	        return {'flagged': False, 'matches': []}
19	    matches = []
20	    text_lower = text.lower()
21	    for kw in KEYWORD_BLOCKLIST:
22	        if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(kw) + r'\b', text_lower):
23	            matches.append(kw)
24	    return {'flagged': len(matches) > 0, 'matches': matches}

27	def auto_flag_review(review_id: int):

118	    report.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
119	    db.session.commit()
120	    return report

123	def file_appeal(report: ModerationReport, appealed_by: User, appeal_text: str) -> ModerationAppeal:
124	    if report.status != ModerationStatus.REVIEW_HIDDEN.value:
125	        raise ValueError("Appeals can only be filed on hidden reviews")
126	    if report.review.reviewer_id != appealed_by.id:
127	        raise PermissionError("Only the review author can file an appeal")
128	    if len(appeal_text) < 20 or len(appeal_text) > 2000:
129	        raise ValueError("Appeal text must be 20-2000 characters")
130	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
131	    resolved_at = report.resolved_at
132	    if resolved_at and resolved_at.tzinfo is None:
133	        resolved_at = resolved_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
134	    if resolved_at:
135	        filing_deadline = resolved_at + timedelta(days=REVIEW_APPEAL_WINDOW_DAYS)
136	        if now > filing_deadline:
137	            raise ValueError("Appeal window has expired. Appeals must be filed within 14 calendar days.")
138	    resolution_deadline = _add_business_days(now, APPEAL_RESOLUTION_DAYS)
139	    appeal = ModerationAppeal(
140	        report_id=report.id,
141	        appealed_by_id=appealed_by.id,
142	        appeal_text=appeal_text,
143	        filed_at=now,
144	        deadline=resolved_at + timedelta(days=REVIEW_APPEAL_WINDOW_DAYS) if resolved_at else now + timedelta(days=REVIEW_APPEAL_WINDOW_DAYS),
145	        resolution_deadline=resolution_deadline,
146	        status=AppealStatus.PENDING.value,
147	    )
148	    db.session.add(appeal)
149	    db.session.commit()
150	    log_action(
151	        action='moderation.appeal',
152	        resource_type='moderation_appeal',
153	        resource_id=appeal.id,
154	        user_id=appealed_by.id,
155	    )
156	    return appeal
157	
158	
159	def resolve_appeal(
160	    appeal: ModerationAppeal,
161	    resolved_by: User,
162	    decision: str,
163	    notes: str | None = None,
164	) -> ModerationAppeal:
165	    if decision not in (AppealStatus.UPHELD.value, AppealStatus.OVERTURNED.value):
166	        raise ValueError("Decision must be 'upheld' or 'overturned'")
167	    appeal.status = decision
168	    appeal.resolved_by_id = resolved_by.id
169	    appeal.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
170	    appeal.resolution_notes = notes
171	    if decision == AppealStatus.OVERTURNED.value:
172	        restore_review(appeal.report, resolved_by)
173	    db.session.commit()
174	    log_action(
175	        action='moderation.appeal_resolve',
176	        resource_type='moderation_appeal',
177	        resource_id=appeal.id,
178	        user_id=resolved_by.id,
179	        new_value={'decision': decision},
180	    )
181	    return appeal
182	
183	
184	def _add_business_days(start: datetime, days: int) -> datetime:
185	    current = start
186	    added = 0
187	    while added < days:
188	        current = current + timedelta(days=1)
189	        if current.weekday() < 5:
190	            added += 1
191	    return current
192

114	def search_drugs(query_text: str = '', form_filter: str = None,
115	                 status_filter: str = None, page: int = 1, per_page: int = 20):
116	    from app.extensions import db as _db
117	    if query_text and query_text.strip():
118	        safe_query = query_text.replace('"', '""')
119	        sql = """
120	            SELECT drug.*, snippet(drug_fts, 0, '<mark>', '</mark>', '...', 30) as snippet_text
121	            FROM drug
122	            JOIN drug_fts ON drug.id = drug_fts.rowid
123	            WHERE drug_fts MATCH :q
124	        """
125	        params = {'q': f'"{safe_query}"'}
126	        if form_filter:
127	            sql += " AND drug.form = :form"
128	            params['form'] = form_filter
129	        if status_filter:
130	            sql += " AND drug.status = :status"
131	            params['status'] = status_filter
132	        else:
133	            sql += " AND drug.status = 'approved'"
134	        sql += " ORDER BY rank LIMIT :limit OFFSET :offset"
135	        params['limit'] = per_page
136	        params['offset'] = (page - 1) * per_page
137	        result = _db.session.execute(_db.text(sql), params)
138	        rows = result.fetchall()
139	        drugs = []
140	        for row in rows:
141	            drug = Drug.query.get(row[0])
142	            drugs.append({'drug': drug, 'snippet': row[-1] if row[-1] else ''})
143	        return {'items': drugs, 'total': len(drugs), 'page': page}
144	    else:
145	        query = Drug.query
146	        if form_filter:
147	            query = query.filter_by(form=form_filter)
148	        if status_filter:
149	            query = query.filter_by(status=status_filter)
150	        else:
151	            query = query.filter_by(status='approved')
152	        total = query.count()
153	        drugs = query.order_by(Drug.generic_name).offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page).all()
154	        return {'items': [{'drug': d, 'snippet': ''} for d in drugs], 'total': total, 'page': page}
155

157	def import_drugs(file_stream, user_id: int) -> dict:
158	    import csv, io
159	    reader = csv.DictReader(io.TextIOWrapper(file_stream, encoding='utf-8'))
160	    imported, skipped, errors = 0, 0, []
161	    for i, row in enumerate(reader, start=2):
162	        try:
163	            gn = row.get('generic_name', '').strip()
164	            strength = row.get('strength', '').strip()
165	            form = row.get('form', '').strip().lower()
166	            if not gn or not strength or not form:
167	                errors.append({'row': i, 'error': 'Missing required field (generic_name, strength, or form)'})
168	                continue
169	            if form not in [f.value for f in DrugForm]:
170	                errors.append({'row': i, 'error': f'Invalid form: {form}'})
171	                continue
172	            exists = Drug.query.filter(
173	                db.func.lower(Drug.generic_name) == gn.lower(),
174	                db.func.lower(Drug.strength) == strength.lower(),
175	                Drug.form == form,
176	            ).first()
177	            if exists:
178	                skipped += 1
179	                continue
180	            drug = Drug(
181	                generic_name=gn,
182	                brand_name=row.get('brand_name', '').strip() or None,
183	                strength=strength,
184	                form=form,
185	                ndc_code=row.get('ndc_code', '').strip() or None,
186	                description=row.get('description', '').strip() or None,
187	                contraindications=row.get('contraindications', '').strip() or None,
188	                side_effects=row.get('side_effects', '').strip() or None,
189	                status=DrugStatus.DRAFT.value,
190	                created_by_id=user_id,
191	            )
192	            db.session.add(drug)
193	            imported += 1
194	        except Exception as e:
195	            errors.append({'row': i, 'error': str(e)})
196	    if imported > 0:
197	        db.session.commit()
198	    log_action(
199	        action='drug.bulk_import',
200	        resource_type='drug',
201	        resource_id=None,
202	        user_id=user_id,
203	        new_value={'imported': imported, 'skipped': skipped, 'errors': len(errors)},
204	    )
205	    return {'imported': imported, 'skipped': skipped, 'errors': errors}
206

25	            return value

27	    except Exception:
28	        return value
29	
30	
31	def _app_decrypt(value: str) -> str:
32	    try:
33	        from flask import current_app
34	        key = current_app.config.get('ENCRYPTION_KEY', '')
35	        if not key:
36	            return value
37	        return decrypt(value, key)
38	    except Exception:
39	        return value
40

45	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
46	    username = db.Column(db.String(64), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)

48	    _email = db.Column('email', db.String(200), nullable=True)

51	        db.String(20),
52	        nullable=False,
53	        default='anonymous',
54	    )
55	    is_active = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False, default=True, server_default='1')
56	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

1	import base64
2	import hashlib
3	import hmac
4	import os
5	import time
6	from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
7	
8	
9	def _load_key(key_b64: str) -> bytes:
10	    """Decode a base64-encoded 32-byte AES key."""
11	    return base64.b64decode(key_b64)
12	
13	
14	def encrypt(plaintext: str, key_b64: str) -> str:
15	    """AES-GCM encrypt plaintext. Returns base64(nonce + ciphertext)."""
16	    key = _load_key(key_b64)
17	    aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
18	    nonce = os.urandom(12)
19	    ct = aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext.encode(), None)
20	    return base64.b64encode(nonce + ct).decode()
21	
22	
23	def decrypt(token: str, key_b64: str) -> str:
24	    """AES-GCM decrypt. Returns plaintext string."""
25	    key = _load_key(key_b64)
26	    aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
27	    raw = base64.b64decode(token)
28	    nonce, ct = raw[:12], raw[12:]
29	    return aesgcm.decrypt(nonce, ct, None).decode()
30	
31	
32	def sign_request(payload: bytes, secret: str, timestamp: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, int]:
33	    """Sign a payload with HMAC-SHA256. Returns (signature_hex, timestamp)."""
34	    if timestamp is None:
35	        timestamp = int(time.time())
36	    msg = f"{timestamp}:".encode() + payload
37	    sig = hmac.new(secret.encode(), msg, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
38	    return sig, timestamp
39	
40	
41	def verify_request(
42	    payload: bytes,
43	    secret: str,
44	    signature: str,
45	    timestamp: int,
46	    window_seconds: int = 300,
47	) -> bool:
48	    """Verify HMAC signature and replay window."""
49	    now = int(time.time())
50	    if abs(now - timestamp) > window_seconds:
51	        return False
52	    expected, _ = sign_request(payload, secret, timestamp)
53	    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, signature)
54	
55	
56	def generate_key() -> str:
57	    """Generate a new base64-encoded 32-byte AES key."""
58	    return base64.b64encode(os.urandom(32)).decode()
59

1	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify
2	from flask_login import login_required, current_user
3	from app.models.training import ClassReview
4	from app.models.moderation import ModerationReport, ModerationAppeal
5	from app.services.moderation_service import (
6	    report_review, hide_review, restore_review, finalize_report, file_appeal, resolve_appeal
7	)
8	from app.utils.decorators import require_permission
9	
10	moderation_bp = Blueprint('moderation', __name__, url_prefix='/api/moderation')
11	
12	
13	@moderation_bp.route('/reports', methods=['POST'])
14	@login_required
15	def create_report():
16	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
17	    review_id = data.get('review_id')
18	    reason = data.get('reason', '').strip()
19	    if not review_id or not reason:
20	        return jsonify({'error': 'review_id and reason are required'}), 400
21	    review = ClassReview.query.get_or_404(review_id)
22	    report = report_review(review, current_user, reason, data.get('keyword_matches'))
23	    return jsonify(report.to_dict()), 201
24	
25	
26	@moderation_bp.route('/reports/<int:report_id>/hide', methods=['POST'])
27	@login_required
28	@require_permission('review.moderate')
29	def hide(report_id: int):
30	    report = ModerationReport.query.get_or_404(report_id)
31	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
32	    report = hide_review(report, current_user, data.get('reason'))
33	    return jsonify(report.to_dict()), 200
34	
35	
36	@moderation_bp.route('/reports/<int:report_id>/restore', methods=['POST'])
37	@login_required
38	@require_permission('review.moderate')
39	def restore(report_id: int):
40	    report = ModerationReport.query.get_or_404(report_id)
41	    report = restore_review(report, current_user)
42	    return jsonify(report.to_dict()), 200
43	
44	
45	@moderation_bp.route('/reports/<int:report_id>/finalize', methods=['POST'])
46	@login_required
47	@require_permission('review.moderate')
48	def finalize(report_id: int):
49	    report = ModerationReport.query.get_or_404(report_id)
50	    report = finalize_report(report, current_user)
51	    return jsonify(report.to_dict()), 200
52	
53	
54	@moderation_bp.route('/appeals', methods=['POST'])
55	@login_required
56	def create_appeal():
57	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
58	    report_id = data.get('report_id')
59	    appeal_text = data.get('appeal_text', '').strip()
60	    if not report_id or not appeal_text:
61	        return jsonify({'error': 'report_id and appeal_text are required'}), 400
62	    report = ModerationReport.query.get_or_404(report_id)
63	    try:
64	        appeal = file_appeal(report, current_user, appeal_text)
65	    except ValueError as e:
66	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
67	    return jsonify(appeal.to_dict()), 201

72	@require_permission('review.moderate')
73	def resolve(appeal_id: int):
74	    appeal = ModerationAppeal.query.get_or_404(appeal_id)
75	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
76	    decision = data.get('decision')
77	    if not decision:
78	        return jsonify({'error': 'decision is required'}), 400
79	    try:
80	        appeal = resolve_appeal(appeal, current_user, decision, data.get('notes'))
81	    except ValueError as e:
82	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
83	    return jsonify(appeal.to_dict()), 200
84

class TestAutoFlag:
    def test_auto_flag_hides_review(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)

class TestModerationReport:
    def test_report_review(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Inappropriate content here')
        assert report.id is not None
        assert report.status == ModerationStatus.PENDING.value

    def test_hide_review(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator, 'Contains spam')
        assert report.status == ModerationStatus.REVIEW_HIDDEN.value
        assert review.is_visible is False

    def test_restore_review(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        restore_review(report, moderator)
        assert review.is_visible is True
        assert report.status == ModerationStatus.REVIEW_RESTORED.value

    def test_duplicate_report_fails(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report_review(review, moderator, 'Inappropriate content here')
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='already reported'):
            report_review(review, moderator, 'Duplicate report attempt here')

    def test_report_reason_too_short(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='10-500 characters'):
            report_review(review, moderator, 'Bad')

    def test_finalize_report(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        finalize_report(report, moderator)
        assert report.status == ModerationStatus.FINALIZED.value
        assert report.resolved_by_id == moderator.id


class TestAppeal:
    def test_file_appeal(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        appeal = file_appeal(report, student, 'My review was valid, please reconsider')
        assert appeal.id is not None
        assert appeal.status == AppealStatus.PENDING.value
        assert appeal.deadline > appeal.filed_at

    def test_resolve_appeal_overturned(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        appeal = file_appeal(report, student, 'Please reconsider this decision')
        resolve_appeal(appeal, moderator, AppealStatus.OVERTURNED.value, 'Review was valid')
        assert appeal.status == AppealStatus.OVERTURNED.value
        assert review.is_visible is True

    def test_resolve_appeal_upheld(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        appeal = file_appeal(report, student, 'My review was valid, please reconsider this decision')
        resolve_appeal(appeal, moderator, AppealStatus.UPHELD.value, 'Content was inappropriate')
        assert appeal.status == AppealStatus.UPHELD.value
        assert review.is_visible is False  # still hidden when upheld

    def test_file_appeal_past_deadline(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        report.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=15)
        _db.session.commit()
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='expired'):
            file_appeal(report, student, 'My review was valid, please reconsider this')

    def test_file_appeal_non_author(self, db):
        student, moderator, review = _setup(db)
        report = report_review(review, moderator, 'Spam content here')
        hide_review(report, moderator)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match='Only the review author'):
            file_appeal(report, moderator, 'My review was valid, please reconsider this!')"}

{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/task-8/clinical_ops_portal/unit_tests/test_encryption_service.py", "content": "import base64
import pytest
from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db as _db
from app.utils.crypto import encrypt, decrypt

# A valid base64-encoded 32-byte key for AES-GCM
VALID_KEY = base64.b64encode(b'\xab' * 32).decode()


@pytest.fixture(scope='function')
def app():
    application = create_app('testing')
    with application.app_context():
        _db.create_all()
        yield application
        _db.session.remove()
        _db.drop_all()


@pytest.fixture(scope='function')
def encrypted_app():
    """App with ENCRYPTION_KEY configured."""
    application = create_app('testing')
    application.config['ENCRYPTION_KEY'] = VALID_KEY
    with application.app_context():
        _db.create_all()
        yield application
        _db.session.remove()
        _db.drop_all()


@pytest.fixture
def db(app):
    return _db


class TestCryptoFunctions:
    def test_encrypt_decrypt_roundtrip(self):
        plaintext = 'hello@example.com'
        token = encrypt(plaintext, VALID_KEY)
        result = decrypt(token, VALID_KEY)
        assert result == plaintext

    def test_different_ciphertext_each_time(self):
        """Same plaintext produces different ciphertext due to random nonces."""
        plaintext = 'same@example.com'
        token1 = encrypt(plaintext, VALID_KEY)
        token2 = encrypt(plaintext, VALID_KEY)
        assert token1 != token2

    def test_decrypt_wrong_key_fails(self):
        """Decrypting with a different key raises an exception."""
        other_key = base64.b64encode(b'\xff' * 32).decode()
        token = encrypt('secret', VALID_KEY)
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            decrypt(token, other_key)

    def test_decrypt_tampered_data_fails(self):
        """Decrypting tampered ciphertext raises an exception."""
        token = encrypt('secret data', VALID_KEY)
        raw = base64.b64decode(token)
        tampered = base64.b64encode(raw[:-1] + bytes([raw[-1] ^ 0xFF])).decode()
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            decrypt(tampered, VALID_KEY)


class TestUserEmailEncryption:
    def test_email_stored_encrypted(self, encrypted_app):
        from app.models.user import User
        user = User(username='alice')
        user.set_password('Secret1!')
        user.email = 'alice@example.com'
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        raw = _db.session.execute(
            _db.text('SELECT email FROM user WHERE id = :id'), {'id': user.id}
        ).scalar()
        assert raw != 'alice@example.com'

    def test_email_decrypts_correctly(self, encrypted_app):
        from app.models.user import User
        user = User(username='bob')
        user.set_password('Secret1!')
        user.email = 'bob@example.com'
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        _db.session.expire(user)
        assert user.email == 'bob@example.com'

    def test_different_ciphertext_per_user(self, encrypted_app):
        """Two users with same email get different stored ciphertext."""
        from app.models.user import User
        u1 = User(username='carol')
        u1.set_password('Secret1!')
        u1.email = 'same@example.com'
        u2 = User(username='dave')
        u2.set_password('Secret1!')
        u2.email = 'same@example.com'
        _db.session.add_all([u1, u2])
        _db.session.commit()
        raw1 = _db.session.execute(
            _db.text('SELECT email FROM user WHERE id = :id'), {'id': u1.id}
        ).scalar()
        raw2 = _db.session.execute(
            _db.text('SELECT email FROM user WHERE id = :id'), {'id': u2.id}
        ).scalar()
        assert raw1 != raw2

    def test_no_key_stores_plaintext(self, app):
        from app.models.user import User
        user = User(username='frank')
        user.set_password('Secret1!')
        user.email = 'frank@example.com'
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        assert user.email == 'frank@example.com'
"}

from datetime import date


def _listing_payload(org_unit_id):
    return {
        'title': 'Studio Apt',
        'address_line1': '10 Elm St',
        'city': 'Chicago',
        'state': 'IL',
        'zip_code': '60601',
        'monthly_rent_cents': 90000,
        'deposit_cents': 90000,
        'lease_start': '2026-07-01',
        'lease_end': '2027-06-30',
        'org_unit_id': org_unit_id,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def org_unit_id(db):
    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
    org = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='TC1').first()
    return org.id


class TestListingAPI:
    def test_create_requires_auth(self, client, org_unit_id):
        resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_create_listing(self, client, org_unit_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        data = resp.get_json()
        assert data['title'] == 'Studio Apt'
        assert data['status'] == 'draft'

    def test_list_listings(self, client):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.get('/api/listings')
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert isinstance(resp.get_json(), list)

    def test_get_listing(self, client, org_unit_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        create_resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        listing_id = create_resp.get_json()['id']
        resp = client.get(f'/api/listings/{listing_id}')
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_update_listing(self, client, org_unit_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        create_resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        listing_id = create_resp.get_json()['id']
        resp = client.put(f'/api/listings/{listing_id}', json={'title': 'Updated Apt'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.get_json()['title'] == 'Updated Apt'

    def test_update_listing_status(self, client, org_unit_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        create_resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        listing_id = create_resp.get_json()['id']
        # Must go through pending_review before published
        client.post(f'/api/listings/{listing_id}/status', json={'status': 'pending_review'})
        resp = client.post(f'/api/listings/{listing_id}/status', json={'status': 'published'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.get_json()['status'] == 'published'

    def test_invalid_status_transition(self, client, org_unit_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        create_resp = client.post('/api/listings', json=_listing_payload(org_unit_id))
        listing_id = create_resp.get_json()['id']
        # draft → published directly is not allowed
        resp = client.post(f'/api/listings/{listing_id}/status', json={'status': 'published'})
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_get_nonexistent_listing(self, client):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.get('/api/listings/99999')
        assert resp.status_code == 404
"}

from datetime import date


@pytest.fixture
def training_class_id(db, client):
    from app.models.user import User
    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
    from app.models.training import TrainingClass, ClassAttendee
    from app.extensions import db as _db
    org = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='TC1').first()
    instructor = User.query.filter_by(username='admin').first()
    staff = User.query.filter_by(username='staffuser').first()
    tc = TrainingClass(
        title='API Test Class',
        instructor_id=instructor.id,
        org_unit_id=org.id,
        class_date=date(2026, 8, 1),
        location='Hall A',
        max_attendees=50,
    )
    _db.session.add(tc)
    _db.session.flush()
    attendee = ClassAttendee(class_id=tc.id, user_id=staff.id, attended=True)
    _db.session.add(attendee)
    _db.session.commit()
    return tc.id


class TestReviewAPI:
    def test_create_review(self, client, training_class_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'staffuser', 'password': 'staffpass'})
        resp = client.post(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews', json={
            'rating': 4,
            'comment': 'Great class content and delivery!',
            'tags': ['engaging'],
        })
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        assert resp.get_json()['rating'] == 4

    def test_list_reviews(self, client, training_class_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.get(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews')
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert isinstance(resp.get_json(), list)

    def test_duplicate_review(self, client, training_class_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'staffuser', 'password': 'staffpass'})
        client.post(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews', json={
            'rating': 3,
            'comment': 'Decent class for all levels.',
        })
        resp = client.post(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews', json={'rating': 5})
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_unauthenticated_cannot_review(self, client, training_class_id):
        resp = client.post(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews', json={'rating': 5})
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_add_coach_reply(self, client, training_class_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'staffuser', 'password': 'staffpass'})
        review_resp = client.post(f'/api/classes/{training_class_id}/reviews', json={
            'rating': 5,
            'comment': 'Excellent class well organized!',
        })
        review_id = review_resp.get_json()['id']
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.post(f'/api/reviews/{review_id}/reply', json={'body': 'Thank you for the feedback!'})
        assert resp.status_code == 201
"}

from datetime import date


@pytest.fixture
def review_id(db, client):
    from app.models.user import User
    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
    from app.models.training import TrainingClass, ClassAttendee
    from app.extensions import db as _db
    from app.services.review_service import create_review
    org = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='TC1').first()
    instructor = User.query.filter_by(username='admin').first()
    staff = User.query.filter_by(username='staffuser').first()
    tc = TrainingClass(
        title='Mod Test Class',
        instructor_id=instructor.id,
        org_unit_id=org.id,
        class_date=date(2026, 9, 1),
        location='Room 3',
        max_attendees=20,
    )
    _db.session.add(tc)
    _db.session.flush()
    attendee = ClassAttendee(class_id=tc.id, user_id=staff.id, attended=True)
    _db.session.add(attendee)
    _db.session.commit()
    review = create_review(tc.id, staff, {'rating': 1, 'comment': 'Absolute waste of time entirely!'})
    return review.id


class TestModerationAPI:
    def test_report_review(self, client, review_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        resp = client.post('/api/moderation/reports', json={
            'review_id': review_id,
            'reason': 'Inappropriate language',
        })
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        return resp.get_json()['id']

    def test_hide_review(self, client, review_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        report_resp = client.post('/api/moderation/reports', json={
            'review_id': review_id,
            'reason': 'Spam content',
        })
        report_id = report_resp.get_json()['id']
        resp = client.post(f'/api/moderation/reports/{report_id}/hide', json={'reason': 'spam'})
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.get_json()['status'] == 'review_hidden'

    def test_restore_review(self, client, review_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        report_resp = client.post('/api/moderation/reports', json={
            'review_id': review_id,
            'reason': 'Spam content',
        })
        report_id = report_resp.get_json()['id']
        client.post(f'/api/moderation/reports/{report_id}/hide', json={'reason': 'spam'})
        resp = client.post(f'/api/moderation/reports/{report_id}/restore')
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert resp.get_json()['status'] == 'review_restored'

    def test_file_appeal(self, client, review_id):
        client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin123'})
        report_resp = client.post('/api/moderation/reports', json={
            'review_id': review_id,
            'reason': 'Spam content',
        })
        report_id = report_resp.get_json()['id']
        client.post(f'/api/moderation/reports/{report_id}/hide', json={'reason': 'spam'})

14	@login_required
15	def list_drugs():
16	    q = request.args.get('q')
17	    if q:
18	        drugs = search_drugs(q, limit=request.args.get('limit', 50, type=int))
19	    else:
20	        status = request.args.get('status')
21	        query = Drug.query
22	        if status:
23	            query = query.filter_by(status=status)
24	        drugs = query.order_by(Drug.generic_name).limit(
25	            request.args.get('limit', 50, type=int)
26	        ).offset(request.args.get('offset', 0, type=int)).all()
27	    return jsonify([d.to_dict() for d in drugs]), 200
28	
29	
30	@drug_bp.route('', methods=['POST'])
31	@login_required
32	@require_permission('drug.create')
33	def create():
34	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
35	    try:
36	        drug = create_drug(data, current_user)
37	    except (ValueError, KeyError) as e:
38	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
39	    return jsonify(drug.to_dict()), 201
40

45	    drug = Drug.query.get_or_404(drug_id)
46	    return jsonify(drug.to_dict()), 200
47	
48

55	    try:
56	        drug = update_drug(drug, data, current_user)
57	    except ValueError as e:
58	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
59	    return jsonify(drug.to_dict()), 200

    # Clinical Operations Portal

    A Flask + SQLite + HTMX web application for managing internal property listings, clinical training classes, and drug knowledge base for a multi-campus healthcare organization.

    ## Tech Stack
    - Backend: Flask 3.1, SQLAlchemy, SQLite (WAL mode, FTS5)
    - Frontend: HTMX 1.9, custom CSS (no framework)
    - Security: AES-GCM encryption, HMAC request signing, RBAC
    - Deployment: Docker Compose

    ## Quick Start
    ```
    docker compose up --build
    ```
    Access: http://localhost:5000
    Default admin: admin / Admin123!

    ## Features
    - Property listing management with publish/review workflow
    - Training class registration and star-rating reviews
    - Drug knowledge base with full-text search and CSV import
    - Content moderation with appeals (14-day window, 5 business day resolution)
    - Multi-level RBAC (campus/college/department/section hierarchy)
    - Temporary permission grants with auto-expiry
    - Immutable audit logs
    - Offline-capable (service worker, stale-while-revalidate caching)
    - Encrypted backups with 30-day retention

    ## Running Tests
    ```
    docker compose exec web bash run_tests.sh
    ```
    Or locally:
    ```
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    bash run_tests.sh
    ```

    ## API Endpoints
    [List all API endpoints with methods]

    ## Environment Variables
    | Variable | Description | Default |
    |----------|-------------|---------|
    | SECRET_KEY | Flask secret key | (required) |
    | HMAC_SECRET | API signing secret | (required) |
    | ENCRYPTION_KEY | AES-GCM key (base64) | (required) |
    | DATABASE_URL | SQLite path | sqlite:///data/clinical_ops.db |

    ## Disaster Recovery
    - RPO: 24 hours (nightly backups)
    - RTO: 4 hours (restore and restart)
    - Backups retained for 30 days
    - Restore: decrypt backup, replace SQLite file, restart container

### 1. **app/api/__init__.py**

- **Status**: Present

  - Roles (org_admin, property_manager, instructor, content_moderator, staff)
  - Organization hierarchy (Campus → College → Department → Section)
  - Default admin user (username: admin, password: admin123)

### 3. **Dockerfile**
- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: Multi-stage Python 3.11-slim container that:
  - Installs gcc dependency
  - Sets up Flask app with gunicorn
  - Exposes port 5000
  - Runs gunicorn with 2 workers and 120s timeout

### 4. **docker-compose.yml**
- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: Single web service that:
  - Builds from Dockerfile
  - Maps port 5000
  - Uses sqlite:////app/data/clinical_ops.db
  - Runs db-init, db-seed, and gunicorn commands
  - Persists data with named volume

### 5. **run_tests.sh**
- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: Bash test runner script that:
  - Runs unit_tests/ and API_tests/ directories with pytest
  - Reports test counts and summary
  - Exits with appropriate status code

### 6. **requirements.txt**
- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: 11 dependencies:
  - Flask 3.1.0, Flask-SQLAlchemy 3.1.1, Flask-Login 0.6.3, Flask-WTF 1.2.2
  - Werkzeug 3.1.3, gunicorn 23.0.0, python-dotenv 1.1.0
  - cryptography 44.0.2, pytest 8.3.5, pytest-flask 1.3.0

### 7. **README.md**
- **Status**: Present

  - Project structure overview
  - Test running instructions

- **Status**: Present

  - **CoachReply**: Instructor reply to class reviews

- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: 3 SQLAlchemy models:
  - **PropertyListing**: Property listing with encrypted address fields, rent/deposit in cents, lease dates, status tracking, and status history
  - **ListingAmenity**: Amenities associated with listings
  - **ListingStatusHistory**: Audit trail of status changes with reason and changed_by user

### 10. **app/models/moderation.py**
- **Status**: Present
- **Contents**: 2 SQLAlchemy models:
  - **ModerationReport**: Reports on reviews with keyword matches, status, and resolution tracking
  - **ModerationAppeal**: Appeals against moderation decisions with filing/deadline dates and resolution tracking

### 11. **app/config.py**
- **Status**: Present

  - **ProductionConfig**: Environment-based database, secure cookies

18	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

20	    instructor = db.relationship('User', foreign_keys=[instructor_id])
21	    org_unit = db.relationship('OrgUnit')
22	    attendees = db.relationship('ClassAttendee', back_populates='training_class', cascade='all, delete-orphan')

1	import sys
2	import os
3	
4	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
5	
6	
7	def seed():
8	    from app import create_app
9	    from app.extensions import db
10	    from app.models.user import User, Role, Permission, user_roles, role_permissions
11	    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
12	    from app.utils.constants import DEFAULT_ROLES, DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS, OrgUnitLevel
13	
14	    app = create_app(os.environ.get('FLASK_ENV', 'development'))
15	    with app.app_context():
16	        db.create_all()
17	
18	        from app.__init__ import _create_fts_table
19	        _create_fts_table(db)
20	
21	        # Seed permissions
22	        perms = {}
23	        for pdata in DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS:
24	            p = Permission.query.filter_by(codename=pdata['codename']).first()
25	            if not p:
26	                p = Permission(**pdata)
27	                db.session.add(p)
28	            perms[pdata['codename']] = p
29	        db.session.flush()
30	
31	        # Seed roles
32	        roles = {}
33	        for rdata in DEFAULT_ROLES:
34	            r = Role.query.filter_by(name=rdata['name']).first()
35	            if not r:
36	                r = Role(**rdata)
37	                db.session.add(r)
38	            roles[rdata['name']] = r
39	        db.session.flush()
40	
41	        # Assign all permissions to org_admin
42	        org_admin = roles['org_admin']
43	        for perm in perms.values():
44	            if perm not in list(org_admin.permissions):
45	                org_admin.permissions.append(perm)
46	
47	        # Assign relevant permissions to property_manager
48	        pm = roles['property_manager']
49	        for codename in ['listing.create', 'listing.edit', 'listing.publish', 'listing.delete']:
50	            if perms[codename] not in list(pm.permissions):
51	                pm.permissions.append(perms[codename])
52	
53	        # Assign relevant permissions to instructor
54	        instructor = roles['instructor']
55	        for codename in ['review.reply']:
56	            if perms[codename] not in list(instructor.permissions):
57	                instructor.permissions.append(perms[codename])
58	
59	        # Assign relevant permissions to content_moderator
60	        moderator = roles['content_moderator']
61	        for codename in ['review.moderate']:
62	            if perms[codename] not in list(moderator.permissions):
63	                moderator.permissions.append(perms[codename])
64	
65	        # Assign basic permissions to staff
66	        staff = roles['staff']
67	        for codename in ['review.create', 'listing.create']:
68	            if perms[codename] not in list(staff.permissions):
69	                staff.permissions.append(perms[codename])
70	
71	        db.session.flush()
72	
73	        # Create sample org hierarchy
74	        campus = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='MAIN').first()
75	        if not campus:
76	            campus = OrgUnit(name='Main Campus', code='MAIN', level=OrgUnitLevel.CAMPUS.value)
77	            db.session.add(campus)
78	            db.session.flush()
79	
80	        college = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='COM').first()
81	        if not college:
82	            college = OrgUnit(
83	                name='College of Medicine', code='COM',
84	                level=OrgUnitLevel.COLLEGE.value, parent_id=campus.id
85	            )
86	            db.session.add(college)
87	            db.session.flush()
88	
89	        dept = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='IM').first()
90	        if not dept:
91	            dept = OrgUnit(
92	                name='Internal Medicine Dept', code='IM',
93	                level=OrgUnitLevel.DEPARTMENT.value, parent_id=college.id
94	            )
95	            db.session.add(dept)
96	            db.session.flush()
97	
98	        section = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='CARD').first()
99	        if not section:
100	            section = OrgUnit(
101	                name='Cardiology Section', code='CARD',
102	                level=OrgUnitLevel.SECTION.value, parent_id=dept.id
103	            )
104	            db.session.add(section)
105	
106	        # Create admin user
107	        admin = User.query.filter_by(username='admin').first()
108	        if not admin:
109	            admin = User(
110	                username='admin',
111	                email='admin@clinical.local',
112	                full_name='System Administrator',
113	                display_preference='full_name',
114	            )
115	            admin.set_password('admin123')
116	            db.session.add(admin)
117	            db.session.flush()
118	            admin.roles.append(roles['org_admin'])
119	
120	        db.session.commit()
121	        print('Seeding complete.')
122	
123	
124	if __name__ == '__main__':
125	    seed()
126

6	    gcc \
7	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
8	
9	COPY requirements.txt .
10	RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
11	
12	COPY . .
13

15	
16	ENV FLASK_APP=app
17	ENV FLASK_ENV=production
18	ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
19	
20	EXPOSE 5000
21	
22	CMD ["gunicorn", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:5000", "--workers", "2", "--timeout", "120", "app:create_app()"]

12	if pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short; then
13	    UNIT_STATUS=0
14	else
15	    UNIT_STATUS=$?
16	fi
17	
18	echo ""
19	echo "========================================"
20	echo "  Running API Tests"
21	echo "========================================"
22	if pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short; then
23	    API_STATUS=0
24	else
25	    API_STATUS=$?
26	fi
27	
28	echo ""
29	echo "========================================"
30	echo "  Test Summary"
31	echo "========================================"
32	
33	# Parse counts using pytest's built-in count output
34	UNIT_RESULT=$(pytest unit_tests/ --tb=no -q 2>&1 | tail -1)
35	API_RESULT=$(pytest API_tests/ --tb=no -q 2>&1 | tail -1)
36	
37	echo "Unit tests : $UNIT_RESULT"
38	echo "API tests  : $API_RESULT"
39	
40	if [ $UNIT_STATUS -ne 0 ] || [ $API_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then
41	    echo ""
42	    echo "RESULT: SOME TESTS FAILED"
43	    exit 1
44	else
45	    echo ""
46	    echo "RESULT: ALL TESTS PASSED"
47	    exit 0
48	fi

8	pages_bp = Blueprint('pages', __name__)
9	
10	
11	@pages_bp.route('/dashboard')
12	@login_required
13	def dashboard():
14	    return render_template('dashboard.html')
15	
16	
17	@pages_bp.route('/admin')
18	@login_required
19	@require_role('org_admin')
20	def admin_panel():
21	    return render_template('admin/index.html')
22	
23	
24	@pages_bp.route('/admin/users')
25	@login_required
26	@require_role('org_admin')
27	def admin_users():
28	    search = request.args.get('search', '').strip()
29	    query = User.query
30	    if search:
31	        query = query.filter(
32	            User.username.ilike(f'%{search}%') | User.full_name.ilike(f'%{search}%')
33	        )
34	    users = query.order_by(User.created_at.desc()).all()
35	    roles = Role.query.all()
36	    if request.headers.get('HX-Request'):
37	        return render_template('admin/partials/user_table.html', users=users, roles=roles)
38	    return render_template('admin/users.html', users=users, roles=roles)
39	
40	
41	@pages_bp.route('/admin/permissions/audit')
42	@login_required
43	@require_role('org_admin')
44	def permission_audit():
45	    start = request.args.get('start_date')
46	    end = request.args.get('end_date')
47	    entries = get_permission_audit_report(start_date=start, end_date=end)
48	    fmt = request.args.get('format')
49	    if fmt == 'csv':
50	        csv_data = export_permission_audit_csv(entries)
51	        return Response(
52	            csv_data,
53	            mimetype='text/csv',
54	            headers={'Content-Disposition': 'attachment; filename=permission_audit.csv'},
55	        )
56	    if request.headers.get('HX-Request'):
57	        return render_template('admin/partials/audit_table.html', entries=entries)
58	    return render_template('admin/permission_audit.html', entries=entries)

2	from flask_login import login_required, current_user
3	from app.extensions import db
4	from app.models.user import User, Role, Permission, TempGrant
5	from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
6	from app.models.audit import AuditLog
7	from app.services.permission_service import assign_role, remove_role, grant_temp_permission, revoke_temp_grant
8	from app.services.audit_service import get_audit_logs
9	from app.services.backup_service import create_backup, list_backups, prune_old_backups

14	admin_bp = Blueprint('admin', __name__, url_prefix='/api/admin')

16	
17	@admin_bp.route('/users', methods=['GET'])
18	@login_required
19	@require_permission('admin.users')
20	def list_users():
21	    users = User.query.order_by(User.username).all()
22	    return jsonify([u.to_dict() for u in users]), 200
23	
24	
25	@admin_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>', methods=['GET'])
26	@login_required
27	@require_permission('admin.users')
28	def get_user(user_id: int):
29	    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
30	    return jsonify(user.to_dict()), 200
31	
32	
33	@admin_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
34	@login_required
35	@require_permission('admin.roles')
36	def add_role(user_id: int):
37	    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
38	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
39	    role = Role.query.filter_by(name=data.get('role')).first_or_404()
40	    assign_role(user, role, current_user.id)
41	    return jsonify(user.to_dict()), 200
42	
43	
44	@admin_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<role_name>', methods=['DELETE'])
45	@login_required
46	@require_permission('admin.roles')
47	def delete_role(user_id: int, role_name: str):
48	    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
49	    role = Role.query.filter_by(name=role_name).first_or_404()
50	    remove_role(user, role, current_user.id)
51	    return jsonify(user.to_dict()), 200
52	
53	
54	@admin_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/temp-grants', methods=['POST'])
55	@login_required
56	@require_permission('permission.grant')
57	def create_temp_grant(user_id: int):
58	    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
59	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
60	    permission = Permission.query.filter_by(codename=data.get('permission')).first_or_404()
61	    hours = data.get('hours', current_app.config.get('TEMP_GRANT_DEFAULT_HOURS', 72))
62	    reason = data.get('reason', '').strip()
63	    if not reason:
64	        return jsonify({'error': 'reason is required'}), 400
65	    grant = grant_temp_permission(user, permission, current_user, reason, hours)
66	    return jsonify(grant.to_dict()), 201
67	
68	
69	@admin_bp.route('/temp-grants/<int:grant_id>/revoke', methods=['POST'])
70	@login_required
71	@require_permission('permission.grant')
72	def revoke_grant(grant_id: int):
73	    grant = TempGrant.query.get_or_404(grant_id)
74	    revoke_temp_grant(grant, current_user)
75	    return jsonify(grant.to_dict()), 200
76	
77	
78	@admin_bp.route('/org-units', methods=['GET'])

80	@require_permission('admin.org_units')
81	def list_org_units():

83	    return jsonify([u.to_dict() for u in units]), 200

85	
86	@admin_bp.route('/org-units', methods=['POST'])

88	@require_permission('admin.org_units')
89	def create_org_unit():
90	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
91	    unit = OrgUnit(
92	        name=data['name'],
93	        code=data['code'],
94	        level=data['level'],
95	        parent_id=data.get('parent_id'),
96	    )
97	    db.session.add(unit)

100	
101	
102	@admin_bp.route('/audit-logs', methods=['GET'])
103	@login_required
104	@require_permission('admin.audit_log')
105	def audit_logs():
106	    logs = get_audit_logs(
107	        resource_type=request.args.get('resource_type'),
108	        resource_id=request.args.get('resource_id', type=int),
109	        user_id=request.args.get('user_id', type=int),
110	        action=request.args.get('action'),
111	        limit=request.args.get('limit', 100, type=int),
112	        offset=request.args.get('offset', 0, type=int),
113	    )
114	    return jsonify([log.to_dict() for log in logs]), 200
115	
116	
117	@admin_bp.route('/backup', methods=['POST'])
118	@login_required
119	@require_permission('admin.backup')
120	def run_backup():
121	    db_uri = current_app.config.get('SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI', '')
122	    db_path = db_uri.replace('sqlite:///', '')
123	    backup_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(db_path), 'backups')
124	    backup_path = create_backup(db_path, backup_dir)
125	    prune_old_backups(backup_dir)
126	    return jsonify({'backup_path': backup_path}), 200
127	
128	
129	@admin_bp.route('/backups', methods=['GET'])

131	@require_permission('admin.backup')

134	    db_path = db_uri.replace('sqlite:///', '')
135	    backup_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(db_path), 'backups')

1	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, render_template, redirect, flash
2	from flask_login import current_user, login_required
3	from app.services.auth_service import register_user, authenticate_user, logout_current_user, change_password
4	from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
5	
6	auth_bp = Blueprint('auth', __name__, url_prefix='/auth')
7	
8	
9	# ── Browser page routes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10	
11	@auth_bp.route('/login', methods=['GET'])
12	def login_page():
13	    if current_user.is_authenticated:
14	        return redirect('/dashboard')
15	    return render_template('auth/login.html')
16	
17	
18	@auth_bp.route('/register', methods=['GET'])
19	def register_page():
20	    if current_user.is_authenticated:
21	        return redirect('/dashboard')
22	    orgs = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
23	    return render_template('auth/register.html', org_units=orgs)
24	
25	
26	# ── Auth API / form handlers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
27	
28	@auth_bp.route('/register', methods=['POST'])
29	def register():
30	    is_form = bool(request.form)
31	    data = request.form if is_form else (request.get_json(silent=True) or {})
32	
33	    username = data.get('username', '').strip()
34	    password = data.get('password', '')
35	    email = data.get('email', '').strip() or None
36	    full_name = data.get('full_name', '').strip() or None
37	
38	    if not username or not password:
39	        msg = 'username and password are required'
40	        if is_form:
41	            flash(msg, 'error')
42	            orgs = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
43	            return render_template('auth/register.html', org_units=orgs), 400
44	        return jsonify({'error': msg}), 400
45	
46	    if is_form:
47	        confirm = data.get('confirm_password', '')
48	        if password != confirm:
49	            flash('Passwords do not match', 'error')
50	            orgs = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
51	            return render_template('auth/register.html', org_units=orgs), 400
52	
53	    org_unit_ids = []
54	    raw_org = data.get('org_unit_id')
55	    if raw_org:
56	        try:
57	            org_unit_ids = [int(raw_org)]
58	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
59	            pass
60	
61	    try:
62	        user = register_user(
63	            username=username,
64	            password=password,
65	            email=email,
66	            full_name=full_name,
67	            org_unit_ids=org_unit_ids or None,
68	        )
69	    except ValueError as e:
70	        if is_form:
71	            flash(str(e), 'error')
72	            orgs = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
73	            return render_template('auth/register.html', org_units=orgs), 400
74	        status = 409 if 'already' in str(e) else 400
75	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), status
76	
77	    if is_form:
78	        flash('Account created. Please sign in.', 'success')
79	        return redirect('/auth/login')
80	    return jsonify(user.to_dict()), 201
81	
82	
83	@auth_bp.route('/login', methods=['POST'])
84	def login():
85	    is_form = bool(request.form)
86	    data = request.form if is_form else (request.get_json(silent=True) or {})
87	
88	    user = authenticate_user(
89	        username=data.get('username', ''),
90	        password=data.get('password', ''),
91	        ip_address=request.remote_addr,
92	    )
93	
94	    if not user:
95	        if is_form:
96	            flash('Invalid credentials', 'error')
97	            return render_template('auth/login.html'), 401
98	        return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid credentials'}), 401
99	
100	    if is_form:
101	        flash('Welcome back!', 'success')
102	        return redirect('/dashboard')
103	    return jsonify(user.to_dict()), 200
104	
105	
106	@auth_bp.route('/logout', methods=['POST'])
107	@login_required
108	def logout():
109	    logout_current_user(user_id=current_user.id)
110	    return jsonify({'message': 'Logged out'}), 200
111	
112	
113	@auth_bp.route('/me', methods=['GET'])
114	@login_required
115	def me():
116	    return jsonify(current_user.to_dict()), 200
117	
118	
119	@auth_bp.route('/change-password', methods=['POST'])
120	@login_required
121	def change_pw():
122	    data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
123	    try:
124	        change_password(current_user, data.get('old_password', ''), data.get('new_password', ''))
125	    except ValueError as e:
126	        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
127	    return jsonify({'message': 'Password updated'}), 200
128

1	from datetime import datetime, timezone
2	from app.extensions import db
3	from app.utils.constants import OrgUnitLevel
4	
5	
6	class OrgUnit(db.Model):
7	    __tablename__ = 'org_unit'
8	
9	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
10	    name = db.Column(db.String(120), nullable=False)
11	    code = db.Column(db.String(32), unique=True, nullable=False)
12	    level = db.Column(db.String(20), nullable=False)
13	    parent_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('org_unit.id'), nullable=True)
14	    is_active = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False, default=True)
15	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
16	
17	    parent = db.relationship('OrgUnit', remote_side=[id], back_populates='children')
18	    children = db.relationship('OrgUnit', back_populates='parent', cascade='all, delete-orphan')
19	    members = db.relationship('UserOrgUnit', back_populates='org_unit', cascade='all, delete-orphan')
20	
21	    def get_ancestors(self) -> list['OrgUnit']:
22	        ancestors = []
23	        current = self.parent
24	        while current is not None:
25	            ancestors.append(current)
26	            current = current.parent
27	        return ancestors
28	
29	    def get_descendants(self) -> list['OrgUnit']:
30	        result = []
31	        stack = list(self.children)
32	        while stack:
33	            node = stack.pop()
34	            result.append(node)
35	            stack.extend(node.children)
36	        return result
37	
38	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
39	        return {
40	            'id': self.id,
41	            'name': self.name,
42	            'code': self.code,
43	            'level': self.level,
44	            'parent_id': self.parent_id,
45	            'is_active': self.is_active,
46	            'created_at': self.created_at.isoformat() if self.created_at else None,
47	        }
48	
49	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
50	        return f'<OrgUnit {self.code}: {self.name}>'
51	
52	
53	class UserOrgUnit(db.Model):
54	    __tablename__ = 'user_org_unit'
55

57	    org_unit_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('org_unit.id'), primary_key=True)
58	    is_primary = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False, default=False)
59	
60	    user = db.relationship('User', back_populates='org_memberships')
61	    org_unit = db.relationship('OrgUnit', back_populates='members')
62

65	            'user_id': self.user_id,
66	            'org_unit_id': self.org_unit_id,
67	            'is_primary': self.is_primary,

content.text:
1	# Clinical Operations Portal
2	
3	A Flask + SQLite + HTMX web application for a multi-campus healthcare organization, providing property listing management, training class reviews with moderation, drug formulary management, role-based access control, and a full audit trail.
4	
5	## Tech Stack
6	
7	- **Backend**: Python 3.11, Flask 3.x, Flask-SQLAlchemy, Flask-Login, Flask-WTF
8	- **Database**: SQLite (WAL mode) with FTS5 full-text search
9	- **Frontend**: HTMX, vanilla CSS, Service Worker for offline support
10	- **Security**: AES-GCM encryption, HMAC request signing, CSRF protection
11	- **Infrastructure**: Docker, Docker Compose, Gunicorn
12	
13	## Prerequisites
14	
15	- [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) (v24+)
16	- [Docker Compose](https://docs.docker.com/compose/install/) (v2+)
17	
18	## Running the Application
19	
20	```bash
21	cp .env.example .env
22	# Edit .env to set SECRET_KEY, HMAC_SECRET, and ENCRYPTION_KEY
23	docker compose up
24	```
25	
26	The application will be available at **http://localhost:5000**
27	
28	Default credentials: `admin` / `admin123`
29	
30	## Running Tests
31	
32	```bash
33	docker compose exec web bash run_tests.sh
34	```
35	
36	## Project Structure
37	
38	```
39	clinical_ops_portal/
40	├── app/
41	│   ├── __init__.py          # App factory
42	│   ├── config.py            # Dev/Test/Prod config
43	│   ├── extensions.py        # SQLAlchemy, LoginManager, CSRF
44	│   ├── models/              # SQLAlchemy models
45	│   ├── services/            # Business logic layer
46	│   ├── api/                 # Flask Blueprints (REST endpoints)
47	│   ├── templates/           # Jinja2 + HTMX templates
48	│   ├── static/              # CSS, JS, Service Worker
49	│   └── utils/               # Validators, decorators, crypto, constants
50	├── scripts/seed_data.py     # Seeds roles, permissions, org hierarchy
51	├── unit_tests/              # Service and model unit tests
52	├── API_tests/               # HTTP endpoint integration tests
53	├── Dockerfile
54	├── docker-compose.yml
55	└── run_tests.sh
56	```
57

1	from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect, flash, current_app
2	from flask_login import login_required, current_user
3	from app.models.training import TrainingClass, ClassAttendee, ClassReview
4	from app.models.organization import OrgUnit
5	from app.services.review_service import (
6	    create_training_class, register_for_class, mark_attendance,
7	    create_review, add_coach_reply, get_reviews_for_class,
8	)
9	
10	class_pages_bp = Blueprint('class_pages', __name__, url_prefix='/classes')
11	
12	
13	@class_pages_bp.route('')
14	@login_required
15	def index():
16	    search = request.args.get('search', '')
17	    query = TrainingClass.query
18	    if search:
19	        query = query.filter(TrainingClass.title.ilike(f'%{search}%'))
20	    classes = query.order_by(TrainingClass.class_date.desc()).all()
21	    if request.headers.get('HX-Request'):
22	        return render_template('classes/partials/class_list.html', classes=classes)
23	    return render_template('classes/index.html', classes=classes, search=search)
24	
25	
26	@class_pages_bp.route('/new')
27	@login_required
28	def new():
29	    orgs = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
30	    return render_template('classes/form.html', org_units=orgs)
31	
32	
33	@class_pages_bp.route('', methods=['POST'])
34	@login_required
35	def create():
36	    data = {
37	        'title': request.form.get('title'),
38	        'description': request.form.get('description'),
39	        'class_date': request.form.get('class_date'),
40	        'location': request.form.get('location'),
41	        'max_attendees': request.form.get('max_attendees', 30),
42	        'org_unit_id': int(request.form.get('org_unit_id')),
43	    }
44	    try:
45	        tc = create_training_class(data, current_user)
46	        flash('Class created', 'success')
47	        return redirect(f'/classes/{tc.id}')
48	    except (ValueError, KeyError) as e:
49	        flash(str(e), 'error')
50	        return redirect('/classes/new')
51	
52	
53	@class_pages_bp.route('/<int:class_id>')
54	@login_required
55	def detail(class_id):
56	    tc = TrainingClass.query.get_or_404(class_id)
57	    attendees = ClassAttendee.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id).all()
58	    is_registered = any(a.user_id == current_user.id for a in attendees)
59	    is_instructor = (tc.instructor_id == current_user.id)
60	    has_reviewed = ClassReview.query.filter_by(class_id=class_id, reviewer_id=current_user.id).first() is not None
61	    can_review = is_registered and any(a.user_id == current_user.id and a.attended for a in attendees) and not has_reviewed
62	    display_mode = current_app.config.get('REVIEWER_DISPLAY_MODE', 'anonymous')
63	    review_data = get_reviews_for_class(class_id, display_mode=display_mode)
64	    return render_template('classes/detail.html', tc=tc, attendees=attendees,
65	                           is_registered=is_registered, is_instructor=is_instructor,
66	                           can_review=can_review, **review_data)
67	
68	
69	@class_pages_bp.route('/<int:class_id>/register', methods=['POST'])
70	@login_required
71	def register(class_id):
72	    try:
73	        register_for_class(class_id, current_user)
74	        flash('Registered successfully', 'success')
75	    except ValueError as e:
76	        flash(str(e), 'error')
77	    return redirect(f'/classes/{class_id}')
78	
79	
80	@class_pages_bp.route('/<int:class_id>/attendance', methods=['POST'])
81	@login_required
82	def attendance(class_id):
83	    user_ids = [int(uid) for uid in request.form.getlist('attended_users')]
84	    try:
85	        mark_attendance(class_id, user_ids, current_user)
86	        flash('Attendance updated', 'success')
87	    except PermissionError as e:
88	        flash(str(e), 'error')
89	    return redirect(f'/classes/{class_id}')
90	
91	
92	@class_pages_bp.route('/<int:class_id>/reviews', methods=['POST'])
93	@login_required
94	def submit_review(class_id):
95	    data = {
96	        'rating': int(request.form.get('rating', 0)),
97	        'tags': request.form.getlist('tags'),
98	        'comment': request.form.get('comment'),
99	    }
100	    try:
101	        create_review(class_id, current_user, data)
102	        flash('Review submitted', 'success')
103	    except (ValueError, PermissionError) as e:
104	        flash(str(e), 'error')
105	    return redirect(f'/classes/{class_id}')
106	
107	
108	@class_pages_bp.route('/<int:class_id>/reviews/<int:review_id>/reply', methods=['POST'])
109	@login_required
110	def reply_to_review(class_id, review_id):
111	    review = ClassReview.query.get_or_404(review_id)
112	    body = request.form.get('body', '')
113	    try:
114	        add_coach_reply(review, current_user, body)
115	        flash('Reply posted', 'success')
116	    except (ValueError, PermissionError) as e:
117	        flash(str(e), 'error')
118	    return redirect(f'/classes/{class_id}')

import os

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))


def seed():
    from app.extensions import db
    from app.models.user import User, Role, Permission
    from app.models.organization import OrgUnit, UserOrgUnit
    from app.utils.constants import DEFAULT_ROLES, DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS, OrgUnitLevel

    # Seed permissions
    perms = {}
    for pdata in DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS:
        p = Permission.query.filter_by(codename=pdata['codename']).first()
        if not p:
            p = Permission(**pdata)
            db.session.add(p)
        perms[pdata['codename']] = p
    db.session.flush()

    # Seed roles
    roles = {}
    for rdata in DEFAULT_ROLES:
        r = Role.query.filter_by(name=rdata['name']).first()
        if not r:
            r = Role(**rdata)
            db.session.add(r)
        roles[rdata['name']] = r
    db.session.flush()

    # org_admin gets all permissions
    org_admin = roles['org_admin']
    existing_admin_perms = {p.codename for p in org_admin.permissions}
    for perm in perms.values():
        if perm.codename not in existing_admin_perms:
            org_admin.permissions.append(perm)

    # property_manager gets listing permissions
    pm = roles['property_manager']
    existing_pm_perms = {p.codename for p in pm.permissions}
    for codename in ['listing.create', 'listing.edit', 'listing.publish', 'listing.delete', 'listing.lock']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_pm_perms:
            pm.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # instructor gets review.reply
    instr = roles['instructor']
    existing_instr_perms = {p.codename for p in instr.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.reply']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_instr_perms:
            instr.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # content_moderator gets review.moderate
    mod = roles['content_moderator']
    existing_mod_perms = {p.codename for p in mod.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.moderate']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_mod_perms:
            mod.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # staff gets basic permissions
    staff_role = roles['staff']
    existing_staff_perms = {p.codename for p in staff_role.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.create', 'listing.create']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_staff_perms:
            staff_role.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    db.session.flush()

    # Create org hierarchy: Main Campus → College of Medicine → Internal Medicine → Cardiology
    campus = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='MAIN').first()
    if not campus:
        campus = OrgUnit(name='Main Campus', code='MAIN', level=OrgUnitLevel.CAMPUS.value)
        db.session.add(campus)
        db.session.flush()

    college = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='COM').first()
    if not college:
        college = OrgUnit(
            name='College of Medicine', code='COM',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.COLLEGE.value, parent_id=campus.id,
        )
        db.session.add(college)
        db.session.flush()

    dept = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='IM').first()
    if not dept:
        dept = OrgUnit(
            name='Internal Medicine', code='IM',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.DEPARTMENT.value, parent_id=college.id,
        )
        db.session.add(dept)
        db.session.flush()

    section = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='CARD').first()
    if not section:
        section = OrgUnit(
            name='Cardiology', code='CARD',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.SECTION.value, parent_id=dept.id,
        )
        db.session.add(section)
        db.session.flush()

    # Admin user
    admin = User.query.filter_by(username='admin').first()
    if not admin:
        admin = User(
            username='admin',
            email='admin@clinical.local',
            full_name='System Administrator',
            display_preference='full_name',
        )
        admin.set_password('Admin123!')
        db.session.add(admin)
        db.session.flush()
        admin.roles.append(roles['org_admin'])
        db.session.add(UserOrgUnit(user_id=admin.id, org_unit_id=campus.id, is_primary=True))

    # Staff user
    staff_user = User.query.filter_by(username='staff').first()
    if not staff_user:
        staff_user = User(
            username='staff',
            email='staff@clinical.local',
            full_name='Staff Member',
            display_preference='anonymous',
        )
        staff_user.set_password('Staff123!')
        db.session.add(staff_user)
        db.session.flush()
        staff_user.roles.append(roles['staff'])
        db.session.add(UserOrgUnit(user_id=staff_user.id, org_unit_id=campus.id, is_primary=True))

    db.session.commit()
    print('Seeding complete.')


if __name__ == '__main__':
    from app import create_app
    from app import _create_fts_table
    from app.extensions import db

    env = os.environ.get('FLASK_ENV', 'development')
    app = create_app(env)
    with app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
        _create_fts_table(db)
        seed()
"}

    db.session.flush()

    # org_admin gets all permissions
    org_admin = roles['org_admin']
    existing_admin_perms = {p.codename for p in org_admin.permissions}
    for perm in perms.values():
        if perm.codename not in existing_admin_perms:
            org_admin.permissions.append(perm)

    # property_manager gets listing permissions
    pm = roles['property_manager']
    existing_pm_perms = {p.codename for p in pm.permissions}
    for codename in ['listing.create', 'listing.edit', 'listing.publish', 'listing.delete', 'listing.lock']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_pm_perms:
            pm.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # instructor gets review.reply
    instr = roles['instructor']
    existing_instr_perms = {p.codename for p in instr.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.reply']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_instr_perms:
            instr.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # content_moderator gets review.moderate
    mod = roles['content_moderator']
    existing_mod_perms = {p.codename for p in mod.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.moderate']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_mod_perms:
            mod.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    # staff gets basic permissions
    staff_role = roles['staff']
    existing_staff_perms = {p.codename for p in staff_role.permissions}
    for codename in ['review.create', 'listing.create']:
        if codename in perms and codename not in existing_staff_perms:
            staff_role.permissions.append(perms[codename])

    db.session.flush()

    # Create org hierarchy: Main Campus → College of Medicine → Internal Medicine → Cardiology
    campus = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='MAIN').first()
    if not campus:
        campus = OrgUnit(name='Main Campus', code='MAIN', level=OrgUnitLevel.CAMPUS.value)
        db.session.add(campus)
        db.session.flush()

    college = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='COM').first()
    if not college:
        college = OrgUnit(
            name='College of Medicine', code='COM',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.COLLEGE.value, parent_id=campus.id,
        )
        db.session.add(college)
        db.session.flush()

    dept = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='IM').first()
    if not dept:
        dept = OrgUnit(
            name='Internal Medicine', code='IM',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.DEPARTMENT.value, parent_id=college.id,
        )
        db.session.add(dept)
        db.session.flush()

    section = OrgUnit.query.filter_by(code='CARD').first()
    if not section:
        section = OrgUnit(
            name='Cardiology', code='CARD',
            level=OrgUnitLevel.SECTION.value, parent_id=dept.id,
        )
        db.session.add(section)
        db.session.flush()

    # Admin user
    admin = User.query.filter_by(username='admin').first()
    if not admin:
        admin = User(
            username='admin',
            email='admin@clinical.local',
            full_name='System Administrator',
            display_preference='full_name',
        )
        admin.set_password('Admin123!')
        db.session.add(admin)
        db.session.flush()
        admin.roles.append(roles['org_admin'])
        db.session.add(UserOrgUnit(user_id=admin.id, org_unit_id=campus.id, is_primary=True))

    # Staff user
    staff_user = User.query.filter_by(username='staff').first()
    if not staff_user:
        staff_user = User(
            username='staff',
            email='staff@clinical.local',
            full_name='Staff Member',
            display_preference='anonymous',
        )
        staff_user.set_password('Staff123!')
        db.session.add(staff_user)
        db.session.flush()
        staff_user.roles.append(roles['staff'])
        db.session.add(UserOrgUnit(user_id=staff_user.id, org_unit_id=campus.id, is_primary=True))

    db.session.commit()
    print('Seeding complete.')


if __name__ == '__main__':
    from app import create_app
    from app import _create_fts_table
    from app.extensions import db

    env = os.environ.get('FLASK_ENV', 'development')
    app = create_app(env)
    with app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
        _create_fts_table(db)
        seed()
"}

{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/task-8/clinical_ops_portal/run_tests.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

echo "========================================"
echo "  Running Unit Tests"
echo "========================================"
if python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short; then
    UNIT_STATUS=0
else
    UNIT_STATUS=$?
fi

echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  Running API Tests"
echo "========================================"
if python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short; then
    API_STATUS=0
else
    API_STATUS=$?
fi

echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  Test Summary"
echo "========================================"
UNIT_RESULT=$(python -m pytest unit_tests/ --tb=no -q 2>&1 | tail -1)
API_RESULT=$(python -m pytest API_tests/ --tb=no -q 2>&1 | tail -1)

echo "Unit tests : $UNIT_RESULT"
echo "API tests  : $API_RESULT"

if [ $UNIT_STATUS -ne 0 ] || [ $API_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: SOME TESTS FAILED"
    exit 1
else
    echo ""
    echo "RESULT: ALL TESTS PASSED"
    exit 0
fi
"}
